Title: Comparative single-nucleus RNA-seq analysis revealed localized and cell type-specific pathways governing root microbiome interactions

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: ATP-grasp domain-containing translation regulator; eIF2 assembly factor
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Meristem-enriched context via downstream targets; not strongly cell type-specific or WCS417-responsive at transcript level
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Not cell type-specific and not WCS417 responsive at transcript level; implicated in proximal meristem translational program
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Necessary for beneficial WCS417-mediated increase in lateral root number and shoot fresh weight
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis in WCS417 growth-promotion assay; snRNA-seq contextualization
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: 13 translation initiation factors; eIF2 heterotrimer subunits
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss impairs beneficial microbe-mediated growth promotion without reducing WCS417 colonization
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential leverage point to enhance beneficial microbe responsiveness, but broad translation-control role suggests pleiotropy risk and favors precise modulation over constitutive alteration
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Described as a master upstream translational regulator likely controlled post-transcriptionally; activity gated by ATP concentration
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CDC123 is required for WCS417-mediated lateral root and shoot growth promotion. 2. Its loss blocks compatible beneficial interaction despite normal WCS417 colonization. 3. The study links translation initiation control to beneficial microbe-induced growth responses.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Translational regulator; organizer of translation-component condensates
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Meristem-associated context via downstream targets; not strongly cell type-specific or WCS417-responsive at transcript level
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Not cell type-specific and not WCS417 responsive at transcript level; implicated in proximal meristem translational program
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Necessary for beneficial WCS417-mediated increase in lateral root number and shoot fresh weight
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis in WCS417 growth-promotion assay; snRNA-seq contextualization
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: 35 cytosolic ribosome translation initiation, elongation, or release factors
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss impairs beneficial microbe-mediated growth promotion without reducing WCS417 colonization
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for tuning host responsiveness to beneficial microbes, but likely high pleiotropy due to master translation role
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Reported as a master upstream hub regulator whose activity is regulated post-translationally by phase separation into condensates
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HEM1 is required for WCS417-mediated plant growth promotion. 2. Mutants block lateral root and shoot fresh-weight responses despite normal bacterial colonization. 3. The study identifies translational regulation as a host process underlying beneficial root-microbe compatibility.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: WCS417-induced in proximal meristem cluster D genes
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced in proximal meristem cells after WCS417 but not GMI1000
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mutation dampens or blocks WCS417-mediated growth promotion
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: snRNA-seq; T-DNA mutant assay for lateral root growth promotion
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely pleiotropic due to core ribosome role
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Represents translational capacity component associated with beneficial microbe response, but direct breeding use is limited by essential housekeeping function
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of six tested ribosome/translation-related genes whose mutants reduced WCS417 growth-promotion phenotype
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RPL24 is among proximal-meristem ribosome genes induced specifically by WCS417. 2. Its mutant impairs WCS417-mediated lateral root promotion. 3. This supports ribosome biogenesis/function as necessary for beneficial microbe-driven growth.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: WCS417-induced in proximal meristem cluster D genes
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced in proximal meristem cells after WCS417 but not GMI1000
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mutation dampens or blocks WCS417-mediated growth promotion
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely pleiotropic due to core ribosome role
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Low direct engineering tractability due to housekeeping function
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Validated among 13 ribosome assembly-related mutants tested
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RPL4 is induced in the WCS417-specific proximal-meristem translational gene set. 2. Mutation compromises beneficial microbe-mediated growth promotion. 3. Supports a causal role of ribosome function in WCS417-induced growth responses.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: WCS417-induced in proximal meristem cluster D genes
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced in proximal meristem cells after WCS417 but not GMI1000
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mutation dampens or blocks WCS417-mediated growth promotion
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely pleiotropic due to core ribosome role
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Marker of beneficial microbe-responsive translational state rather than ideal direct engineering target
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Part of genetically validated ribosome-related set required for WCS417 effect
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RPS18A is part of the WCS417-induced proximal-meristem translation program. 2. Mutant plants show reduced WCS417-mediated lateral root promotion. 3. Confirms importance of small-subunit ribosome function in beneficial interaction outcomes.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: WCS417-induced in proximal meristem cluster D genes
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced in proximal meristem cells after WCS417 but not GMI1000
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mutation dampens or blocks WCS417-mediated growth promotion
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential broad growth effects due to translation role
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential indicator of beneficial microbe-responsive translation but broad essential function limits direct deployment
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Listed in paper as translation elongation factor-like gene required for WCS417 response
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EF1B is among translation-related genes induced in proximal meristem by WCS417. 2. Its mutant reduces beneficial microbe-mediated growth promotion. 3. Supports translational elongation as part of the compatible response to WCS417.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: WCS417-induced in proximal meristem cluster D genes
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced in proximal meristem cells after WCS417 but not GMI1000
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mutation dampens or blocks WCS417-mediated growth promotion
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential broad growth effects due to translation role
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential biomarker of beneficial microbe-induced translation state; direct editing may be pleiotropic
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EF1β is specifically induced by WCS417 in proximal meristem cells. 2. Mutation compromises growth promotion by WCS417. 3. Beneficial microbe response requires translation elongation machinery.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: WCS417-induced in proximal meristem cluster D genes
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced in proximal meristem cells after WCS417 but not GMI1000
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mutation dampens or blocks WCS417-mediated growth promotion
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential pleiotropy due to broad translation-related functions
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential component of beneficial interaction competence, but direct engineering should be cautious due to housekeeping role
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Validated as one of six genes whose mutation reduces WCS417-mediated growth promotion
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ELF5A-2 belongs to the WCS417-specific proximal-meristem translational response. 2. Mutation reduces the beneficial growth-promoting phenotype. 3. Translation-associated factors are causally required for WCS417-induced development.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms the pathway scaffold leading to target triterpene products and required for microbiome-modulating triterpene output
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step supported genetically for microbiome reshaping, rate-limiting not established
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Selective antimicrobial/root microbiome-sculpting specialized metabolites involved in microbiome reshaping during pathogen infection
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced strongly by GMI1000 in root maturation cells; higher in cortex and atrichoblast than endodermis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced strongly by GMI1000 in root maturation cells; higher in cortex and atrichoblast than endodermis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Ralstonia solanacearum GMI1000 infection; natural soil microbiome context
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for reshaping the root microbiome upon GMI1000 infection; thas1 blocks infection-triggered microbiome restructuring
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: snRNA-seq; promoter-reporter imaging; mutant analysis; natural-soil root microbiome 16S sequencing; PCoA/DESeq2 analyses
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Does not measurably alter Ralstonia resistance in mono-association assay; effect appears on microbiome structure rather than direct pathogen resistance
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to engineer pathogen-responsive microbiome recruitment in roots, but deployment should preserve beneficial microbiome balance and may require promoter tuning rather than constitutive manipulation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: snRNA-seq PRJCA019084; root microbiome 16S sequencing PRJCA026420
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A triterpene biosynthesis mutant specifically blocked microbiome reshaping after pathogen infection; prior work cited indicates THAS1 enriches Arabidopsis-specific root bacteria
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. THAS1 is strongly and specifically induced by GMI1000 in root maturation-zone outer cell types. 2. Reporter imaging validated maturation-zone-enriched pathogen responsiveness. 3. thas1 mutants failed to significantly reshape the root microbiome after GMI1000 infection, establishing THAS1-dependent triterpene biosynthesis as a causal pathway in infection-driven microbiome remodeling.
